name: Narrative Architecture
decision_axis: This lever controls narrative scope by choosing between linear story, branching arcs, and modular episodic segments.
consequences: Pulling this lever reduces narrative complexity, allowing faster iteration and lower design overhead. However, a linear story may limit player agency, while branching or episodic approaches increase design and testing effort. The choice influences how quickly the narrative can be refined and how engaging the story feels to players.
options: Develop a single linear storyline with a fixed protagonist arc that guides players through a cohesive narrative.
Implement branching storylines that allow player choices to alter outcomes, creating multiple narrative paths for.
Create modular episodic chapters that can be developed and released independently, enabling flexible content updates.
review: Balancing narrative depth with development speed; options ignore the integration overhead of episodic modules.

name: World Detail Strategy
decision_axis: This lever controls world detail by choosing between fully procedural, hybrid, or fully handcrafted environments.
consequences: Pulling this lever cuts asset creation time and can lower upfront costs, but may produce less distinctive landmarks and increase computational load during runtime. A fully handcrafted approach ensures artistic uniqueness but requires more artists and longer production cycles. The decision shapes the visual identity and performance profile of the open world.
options: Generate the entire cityscape procedurally using algorithmic zoning to accelerate asset creation and provide diverse environments.
Combine procedural base with handcrafted landmarks to balance efficiency and artistic authenticity for immersive player experience.
Build all city assets manually to ensure artistic control and unique visual identity for player engagement.
review: Balancing realism with resource constraints; options overlook the need for performance optimization in fully procedural approach.

name: Funding Structure
decision_axis: This lever controls financial risk by choosing between publisher-only, grant-only, and hybrid funding.
consequences: Pulling this lever determines the project's cash flow and creative control. Publisher-only funding guarantees a steady budget but may impose deadlines and content guidelines. Grant-only funding preserves autonomy but may limit the scale of the project. A hybrid mix spreads risk but requires managing two funding streams.
options: Secure full funding from a major publisher, accepting their creative guidelines to streamline development and release.
Rely exclusively on government innovation grants, maintaining full creative autonomy while meeting public sector criteria.
Combine publisher investment with grant funding to diversify risk and leverage both financial and strategic resources.
review: Balancing creative freedom with financial stability; options miss potential regulatory constraints on grant usage.

name: Studio Layout
decision_axis: This lever controls collaboration by choosing between centralized office, distributed studio, and hybrid co-location.
consequences: Pulling this lever affects team cohesion and travel costs. A single headquarters centralizes collaboration but may limit geographic talent pools. Multiple studios broaden talent access but can fragment communication. A hybrid model balances local focus with periodic in-person alignment.
options: Establish a single, large headquarters in a major city to house all teams and foster collaboration.
Operate multiple small studios across different regions to tap local talent and diversify creative perspectives.
Use a hybrid model with a central hub and satellite teams that meet quarterly to balance cohesion and flexibility.
review: Balancing talent access with collaboration efficiency; options ignore the impact of regional IP laws on distributed teams.

name: Multiplayer Strategy
decision_axis: This lever controls player engagement by choosing between single-player focus, live-service multiplayer, and hybrid mode.
consequences: Pulling this lever changes revenue streams and ongoing support requirements. A single-player focus reduces long-term maintenance but may limit player retention. A live-service model offers continuous engagement but demands persistent infrastructure and content updates. A hybrid approach attempts to capture both markets but requires careful resource allocation.
options: Focus exclusively on a single-player campaign with limited multiplayer features to prioritize narrative depth for players.
Build a full live-service multiplayer ecosystem with persistent world and events to sustain long-term engagement.
Offer a hybrid mode that supports both a rich single-player story and a seasonal multiplayer expansion to balance resources.
review: Balancing long-term engagement with upfront development cost; options overlook the cost of maintaining live-service infrastructure.

name: Release Cadence
decision_axis: This lever controls market momentum by choosing between single launch, phased rollout, and episodic release.
consequences: Pulling this lever influences marketing spend and player retention. A single launch maximizes initial hype but may leave players without new content quickly. Phased rollout spreads content over time, sustaining interest but diluting early buzz. Episodic release keeps players engaged over a longer period but risks fragmenting the community.
options: Release the entire game in one global launch event to maximize initial hype and sales momentum.
Roll out the game in phases, adding new districts and features over six months to sustain interest.
Deliver the game episodically, releasing a new chapter every quarter to keep players engaged over time.
review: Managing hype versus continuous content; options miss the risk of fragmented player base.

name: Procedural Generation Tuning
decision_axis: This lever controls procedural generation depth by choosing between high-complexity, moderate-complexity, and low-complexity algorithms.
consequences: Pulling this lever increases map variety and reduces design time but may increase memory usage and load times. A high-complexity engine can generate diverse districts with unique landmarks, raising build times. A low-complexity generator speeds up production but limits environmental novelty.
options: Implement a high-complexity procedural engine that generates diverse districts with unique landmarks, increasing replayability but raising build times.
Adopt a moderate-complexity system that balances varied city layouts with predictable performance, ensuring stable frame rates.
Use a low-complexity generator that relies on pre-built tiles, speeding up production but limiting environmental novelty.
review: Balancing map variety against performance risk; options ignore potential GPU bottlenecks during peak load.

name: NPC Interaction Depth
decision_axis: This lever controls NPC interaction richness by choosing between scripted dialogues, dynamic AI-driven conversations, and hybrid systems.
consequences: Pulling this lever enhances player immersion but increases QA complexity and memory footprint. Scripted dialogues cover key story beats, simplifying testing. Dynamic AI-driven conversations adapt to player actions, deepening immersion but requiring extensive behavior tree testing.
options: Deploy fully scripted dialogues that cover key story beats, ensuring narrative consistency while simplifying testing.
Implement dynamic AI-driven conversations that adapt to player actions, deepening immersion but requiring extensive behavior tree testing.
Combine scripted anchors with AI overlays that fill gaps, balancing narrative control with emergent dialogue while adding moderate QA load.
review: Depth versus testability; options overlook the cost of continuous AI tuning during post-launch updates.

name: Vehicle Customization Scope
decision_axis: This lever controls vehicle customization breadth by choosing between limited presets, modular parts, and full user-designed skins.
consequences: Pulling this lever expands player expression but increases art pipeline workload and potential performance issues. Limited presets showcase iconic styles, reducing art effort while maintaining visual impact. Modular parts allow swapping body panels, wheels, and paint, expanding creativity but adding assembly logic.
options: Offer a curated set of limited presets that showcase iconic styles, reducing art effort while maintaining visual impact.
Introduce modular parts that allow players to swap body panels, wheels, and paint, expanding creativity but adding assembly logic.
Enable full user-designed skins through a marketplace, fostering community content while requiring moderation and compatibility checks.
review: Customization breadth versus pipeline complexity; options miss the risk of performance degradation on low-end hardware.

name: Morality System Integration
decision_axis: This lever controls morality system integration by choosing between linear consequence, branching narrative, and emergent reputation mechanics.
consequences: Pulling this lever affects player agency and narrative cohesion, potentially increasing development time. Linear consequence models trigger fixed penalties, simplifying design but limiting choice. Branching narratives map decisions to distinct paths, enriching depth but expanding script requirements.
options: Implement a linear consequence model where each crime triggers a fixed penalty, simplifying design but limiting player choice.
Create a branching narrative that maps each decision to distinct story paths, enriching depth but expanding script requirements.
Develop an emergent reputation system that tracks player actions across factions, generating dynamic responses but demanding complex AI.
review: Agency versus complexity; options ignore the challenge of balancing moral ambiguity with clear player feedback.

name: Dynamic Weather Implementation
decision_axis: This lever controls dynamic weather coverage by choosing between static seasonal cycles, real-time weather simulation, and player-triggered weather events.
consequences: Pulling this lever enhances realism but increases engine load and testing overhead. Static seasonal cycles change lighting and traffic patterns, adding atmosphere with minimal performance cost. Real-time simulation updates cloud cover, precipitation, and wind, boosting immersion but raising GPU usage.
options: Apply static seasonal cycles that change lighting and traffic patterns, adding atmosphere with minimal performance cost.
Integrate real-time weather simulation that updates cloud cover, precipitation, and wind, boosting immersion but raising GPU usage.
Introduce player-triggered weather events that allow in-game triggers to alter conditions, offering tactical options while adding scripting complexity.
review: Realism versus performance; options overlook the impact of weather on rendering pipelines during high-action scenes.

name: Sound Design Immersion
decision_axis: This lever controls sound design depth by choosing between ambient city loops, adaptive music layers, and interactive audio cues.
consequences: Pulling this lever increases player immersion but requires more audio assets and mixing effort. Ambient loops capture traffic, sirens, and crowds, creating a realistic backdrop while limiting dynamic variation. Adaptive music shifts intensity based on player actions, enhancing emotional impact but demanding complex audio middleware.
options: Record ambient city loops that capture traffic, sirens, and crowds, creating a realistic backdrop while limiting dynamic variation.
Layer adaptive music that shifts intensity based on player actions, enhancing emotional impact but demanding complex audio middleware.
Implement interactive audio cues that respond to environmental changes, providing feedback but increasing sound asset count.
review: Immersion versus asset volume; options ignore the difficulty of maintaining consistent audio quality across diverse hardware.

name: City Realism Calibration
decision_axis: This lever controls city realism by choosing between high-fidelity mapping, stylized abstraction, and hybrid procedural overlays.
consequences: Pulling this lever increases the authenticity of the city layout, but may raise asset creation time and storage costs. A hybrid approach can reduce both but may compromise visual consistency.
options: Adopt a high-fidelity mapping strategy that imports satellite imagery and overlays detailed 3D scans of real streets.
Apply a stylized abstraction method that simplifies urban geometry into iconic shapes, reducing polygon count while preserving landmark identity.
Integrate a hybrid procedural overlay that blends scanned data with algorithmic terrain generation, allowing dynamic weather effects to alter street textures in real time.
review: Choosing a high-fidelity mapping increases realism but may inflate development time; the options ignore potential licensing costs for satellite data.

name: Heist Complexity Balancing
decision_axis: This lever controls heist complexity by choosing between linear missions, branching objectives, and emergent chaos systems.
consequences: Pulling this lever increases player agency and replayability, but may complicate QA and increase narrative cohesion risk. Emergent chaos can reduce design overhead but may dilute narrative focus.
options: Design linear heist missions with tightly scripted objectives, ensuring consistent pacing and predictable player progression.
Implement branching objectives that allow players to choose between multiple entry routes, each with distinct rewards and risks.
Create emergent chaos systems where environmental factors and NPC reactions dynamically alter mission parameters, requiring adaptive player strategies.
review: Branching objectives enhance depth but may fragment narrative; the options do not address potential story continuity issues across divergent paths.

name: Dynamic NPC Economy Modeling
decision_axis: This lever controls NPC economic realism by choosing between static asset loops, scripted micro-economies, and AI-driven market dynamics.
consequences: Pulling this lever yields more believable in-game economies, but increases computational load and data complexity. AI-driven dynamics can reduce manual scripting but may introduce unpredictable behavior.
options: Implement static asset loops that cycle through predefined supply and demand charts, keeping NPC transactions predictable and resource-efficient.
Script micro-economies for key districts, allowing merchants to adjust prices based on limited player interactions and seasonal events.
Deploy AI-driven market dynamics that simulate supply shocks, inflation, and black-market fluctuations, requiring real-time data feeds and adaptive NPC behavior.
review: AI-driven market dynamics promise realism but risk performance bottlenecks; the options overlook the need for profiling and optimization of real-time simulations.

name: Modular Vehicle Architecture
decision_axis: This lever controls vehicle customization depth by choosing between limited preset upgrades, modular component swapping, and full physics-based modification.
consequences: Pulling this lever expands player creativity and monetization potential, but may increase testing complexity and balance issues. Full physics-based mods can enhance immersion but require extensive validation.
options: Offer limited preset upgrades that modify vehicle appearance and performance through a curated selection of skins and engine boosts.
Enable modular component swapping, allowing players to attach interchangeable body panels, suspension kits, and engine modules that affect handling.
Implement full physics-based modification, letting players rebuild chassis, tweak aerodynamics, and calibrate torque curves for realistic performance changes.
review: Full physics-based modification increases immersion but may expose balance exploits; the options do not consider rollback or cheat detection mechanisms.

name: Multiplayer Persistence Design
decision_axis: This lever controls multiplayer persistence by choosing between stateless session servers, stateful world servers, and hybrid cloud-hosted persistence.
consequences: Pulling this lever determines data consistency and scalability; stateless servers reduce latency but lose world continuity. Hybrid can balance but adds operational complexity.
options: Deploy stateless session servers that reset the world after each match, ensuring low latency and simplified server management.
Run stateful world servers that maintain persistent player progress and city changes, requiring robust data replication and backup strategies.
Adopt a hybrid cloud-hosted persistence model that stores core world state in the cloud while keeping active sessions on edge servers for performance.
review: Hybrid persistence promises scalability but introduces data consistency challenges; the options ignore potential latency spikes during state synchronization.

name: Funding Milestone Alignment
decision_axis: This lever controls funding alignment by choosing between milestone-based tranche releases, continuous investment streams, and contingency reserve buffers.
consequences: Pulling this lever affects cash flow and risk exposure; tranche releases tie funding to deliverables but may delay cash. Continuous streams provide liquidity but risk overspend; reserves buffer risk but tie up capital.
options: Structure funding into milestone-based tranches that release capital only after approved deliverable reviews, ensuring accountability.
Establish continuous investment streams that provide regular cash injections, allowing teams to maintain momentum without waiting for milestone approvals.
Create contingency reserve buffers that set aside a percentage of total funding for unforeseen scope changes, mitigating risk without impacting scheduled releases.
review: Milestone-based tranches enforce discipline but may slow development; the options do not account for potential delays in deliverable approvals that could stall funding.
